zone_6b_notes: Sow directly in early spring as soon as soil can be worked — mid-March to April in Farmington. Missouri clay must be amended deeply (12+ inches) with compost or sand; rocky or compacted soil causes forked, stubby roots. For fall harvest, sow mid-July to August 1 — carrots sweetened by fall frost are exceptional. Nantes and Danvers types perform best in Missouri soils.
water: consistent — 1 inch/week; irregular watering causes splitting and forked roots
